original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: People prescribed medicines and healthcare professionals who prescribe, dispense, or review medicines.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for non-judgemental adherence history, missed-dose or medicine-taking behaviour documentation, beliefs, concerns, practical barriers, preferred support, and periodic review. It does not recommend a medicine, dose, change, or adherence intervention automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.2 — periodic review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.

title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
population: People prescribed medicines and healthcare professionals who prescribe, dispense, or review medicines.
intended_setting: Shared medicine decisions, adherence assessment, support, and periodic medicine review.
